name: vscode-devtools
description: VS Code DevTools with Git workspace picker, LM tool management, and MCP integration

scripts.mcp:build: cd mcp-server && npm run build && tsc --noEmit

commands.title: VS Code DevTools: Toggle MCP Server

devtools.notifications.duration.description: How long completion notifications stay visible (seconds). 0 = suppress all, -1 = show until dismissed, >0 = auto-dismiss after N seconds.

devtools.dev.enabled.description: Enable dev mode. When enabled, the MCP server is registered and available to Copilot.

devtools.clientWorkspace.description: Path to the client workspace folder (absolute, or relative to host workspace root). If empty, uses the host workspace root.

devtools.extensionPath.description: Path to the vscode-devtools extension folder (absolute, or relative to host workspace root).

devtools.hotReload.enabled.description: Enable automatic hot-reload on source changes.

devtools.launch.skipReleaseNotes.description: Suppress the release-notes tab on client startup.

devtools.launch.skipWelcome.description: Suppress the welcome tab on client startup.

devtools.launch.disableGpu.description: Disable GPU hardware acceleration for the client window.

devtools.launch.disableWorkspaceTrust.description: Disable workspace-trust dialog in the client window.

devtools.launch.verbose.description: Enable verbose VS Code logging for the client window.

devtools.launch.extraArgs.description: Arbitrary extra CLI flags forwarded verbatim to the client VS Code.

devtools.hotReload.mcpStatusTimeout.description: Max wait time (ms) for MCP status tool before timeout.

devtools.inspector.autoRetryOnRestart.description: Automatically retry tool requests after MCP server restarts instead of showing the reboot message.

languageModelTools.name: output_read
languageModelTools.userDescription: Read VS Code output logs from all active sessions — Host and Client (Extension Development Host)
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Read VS Code output logs from all active sessions. When called without a channel, lists all available output channels organized by session (Host and Client) and category. When called with a channel name, returns log content with optional filtering. If the same channel name exists in both sessions, returns both.

**LISTING CHANNELS (no channel provided):**
Returns all available output channels grouped by session (Host, Client) and category (Main Logs, Window Logs, Extension Host, Extension Logs, Output Channels).

**READING CHANNEL CONTENT (channel provided):**
- `session` ('host' | 'client'): Filter to a specific session. Omit for both.
- `limit` (number): Get the N most recent lines. Default: all lines
- `pattern` (string): Regex pattern to match against line content (case-insensitive)
- `afterLine` (number): Only lines after this line number (for incremental reads)
- `beforeLine` (number): Only lines before this line number
- `lineLimit` (number): Max characters per line (truncates with '...')

**DRILL-DOWN PARAMETERS (for exploring compressed log overviews):**
- `templateId` (string): Show raw lines matching this template ID
- `severity` ('error' | 'warning' | 'info'): Filter by severity level
- `timeRange` (string): Time window: 'HH:MM-HH:MM' or 'HH:MM:SS-HH:MM:SS'
- `minDuration` (string): Show templates with durations >= threshold: '1s', '500ms'
- `correlationId` (string): Trace a specific request by UUID
- `includeStackFrames` (boolean): Show/hide stack frame templates. Default: true

**EXAMPLES:**
- List all channels from all sessions: {}
- List only client channels: { session: 'client' }
- Read extension host logs: { channel: 'exthost' }
- Read client-only exthost: { channel: 'exthost', session: 'client' }
- Get last 50 lines: { channel: 'exthost', limit: 50 }
- Find errors: { channel: 'main', pattern: 'error|exception|failed', limit: 100 }

**USE THIS TOOL when:**
- User asks about VS Code logs, output, or errors
- Debugging extension issues in Host or Client windows
- Looking for specific log messages
- Monitoring extension host activity
- Comparing Host vs Client behavior

channel.description: Name of the output channel to read (e.g., 'exthost', 'main', 'Git'). If omitted, lists all available channels.

session.description: Filter to a specific VS Code session. 'host' = main VS Code window, 'client' = Extension Development Host. If omitted, shows both sessions.

limit.description: Get the N most recent lines. Omit to get all lines.

pattern.description: Regex pattern to match against line content (case-insensitive).

afterLine.description: Only return lines with line number greater than this (for incremental reads).

beforeLine.description: Only return lines with line number less than this.

lineLimit.description: Max characters per line. Longer lines are truncated with '...'.

templateId.description: Show raw lines matching this template ID from the compressed overview.

correlationId.description: Trace a specific request by UUID/correlation ID.

includeStackFrames.description: Show/hide stack frame templates. Default: true.

languageModelTools.name: logFile_read
languageModelTools.userDescription: Read and analyze log files with automatic pattern compression
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Read and analyze log files with automatic pattern compression and drill-down filtering.

Detects repeating patterns in log output and compresses them into a structured overview with severity counts, pattern tables, and drill-down capabilities.

**Supported Extensions:**
- Log files: .log, .out, .err, .trace, .syslog, .access, .audit
- Structured logs: .jsonl, .ndjson
- Diagnostic: .dump, .diag, .debug
- Experimental (auto-detected): .txt, .csv

**Parameters:**
- `file` (required) — Path to log file (relative or absolute)
- `templateId` — Expand a specific pattern from the overview
- `severity` — Filter by error, warning, or info
- `timeRange` — Filter by time window (HH:MM-HH:MM)
- `pattern` — Regex filter on raw content
- `minDuration` — Show slow operations (e.g. '1s', '500ms')
- `correlationId` — Trace a specific request UUID
- `includeStackFrames` — Show/hide stack frames (default: true)

**EXAMPLES:**
- Overview: { file: "server.log" }
- Errors only: { file: "server.log", severity: "error" }
- Drill into pattern: { file: "server.log", templateId: "t003" }
- Search: { file: "app.log", pattern: "timeout|connection refused" }
- Slow ops: { file: "perf.log", minDuration: "1s" }

file.description: Path to log file (relative to workspace root or absolute).

templateId.description: Show raw lines matching this template ID from the compressed log overview.

severity.description: Filter compressed logs by severity level.

pattern.description: Regex pattern to filter log content (case-insensitive).

correlationId.description: Trace a specific request by UUID/correlation ID.

includeStackFrames.description: Show/hide stack frame templates. Default: true.

languageModelTools.name: wait
languageModelTools.userDescription: Wait for a specified duration before continuing
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Wait for a specified duration before continuing. Useful for giving the page time to update, animations to complete, or network requests to settle.

Args:
  - durationMs (number): Duration in milliseconds (0-30000)
  - reason (string): Optional explanation for the wait

Returns:
  { elapsed_ms, requested_ms, reason? }

Examples:
  - "Wait for animation" -> { durationMs: 500, reason: "animation to complete" }
  - "Wait for API response" -> { durationMs: 2000, reason: "network request to settle" }

Error Handling:
  - Duration must be between 0 and 30000ms

durationMs.description: Duration to wait in milliseconds. Must be between 0 and 30000 (30 seconds).

reason.description: Optional reason for waiting (e.g., "waiting for animation to complete"). Included in the response for context.

languageModelTools.name: file_delete
languageModelTools.userDescription: Delete a file or folder after verifying no symbols are referenced externally
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Delete a file or folder from the workspace after verifying no external references would break.

For files: queries the language server for all symbols, then checks each for references in other files. If any external references exist, deletion is blocked with a detailed report.

For folders: scans all code files inside the folder, checks each file's symbols for references from files OUTSIDE the folder (internal cross-references within the folder are ignored). If any external references exist, deletion is blocked.

Files and folders are moved to trash for recovery.

Args:
  - filePath (string): Path of the file or folder to delete (absolute or relative to workspace root)

Returns on success: { success: true, deletedFile, fileCount? }
Returns when blocked: { success: false, blocked: true, brokenReferences: [...], message }

Use this tool when asked to delete, remove, or clean up a file or folder.

filePath.description: Path of the file or folder to delete (absolute or relative to workspace root).

languageModelTools.name: file_rename
languageModelTools.userDescription: Rename or move a file or folder, updating all imports and references
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Rename or move a file or folder in the workspace, with optional import/reference updates across the codebase.

When updateRefs=true, uses VS Code's refactoring API to ensure all import paths, re-exports, and references are updated to reflect the new file location. When updateRefs=false, performs a plain rename/move without updating imports or references. Supports both files and folders.

Args:
  - filePath (string): Current path of the file or folder (absolute or relative to workspace root)
  - newPath (string): New path for the file or folder (absolute or relative to workspace root)
  - updateRefs (boolean): Whether to automatically update imports and references after the move

Returns on success: { success: true, oldPath, newPath, filesAffected: [...] }
Returns on failure: { success: false, error: '...' }

Use updateRefs=false for archiving or relocating files when reference updates are not wanted.

filePath.description: Current path of the file or folder to rename (absolute or relative to workspace root).

newPath.description: New path for the file or folder (absolute or relative to workspace root).

updateRefs.description: Whether to automatically update imports and references after the move.
